h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Three tiles to this bar line, so the top label stays 3. With this card in the lower box, the lower label changes to 8. No fourth tile gets added.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on track to keep confirming the top/bottom separation with the same quarter-note card, so the expected next step was another identical confirmation.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Three marks in this bar, lower box takes 8 from the card. So 3/8 is three eighth-note beats, not a fourth count. Does the swapped card decide the note drawn under each tile, or only the lower number?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 physically draws eighth notes under each tile, applies the new beat-unit card to produce a new time signature (3/8), and formulates a genuine discriminating question about the card's dual role — performing a new representational task rather than merely repeating the established separation.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Use that as the check: top number counts these tiles in one bar; bottom number names this card as the beat unit. Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed. Speed stays off this pair of boxes.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T1 responds to L1's confusion by introducing a cover-and-uncover procedural check that separates the two box jobs, which is a useful scaffolding move, but it narrows and clarifies the same conceptual route rather than making a legible stock-taking contrast against a failed prior route, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Top number, bottom number: one bar, one beat unit. Keep the tiles fixed: the card changes the lower number and the beat mark drawn under each tile; it adds no fourth beat. Three tiles, three eighth-note beats. Take that count into the entry.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 directly answers L4's discriminating question about the card's dual role (lower number and beat mark drawn), which is responsive to the learner's revised framing, but it continues the same representational route rather than visibly changing task, evidence standard, or framing move.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue methodically naming the acting object and placing each card in sequence, following the tutor's established protocol.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence:
Earth on cart: down.
The up arrow is track on cart.
Track card goes there; the track pushes up on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently applies the object-first criterion to the previously unaddressed blank arrow, naming the track as the acting object and supplying direction without tutor prompting, showing the device has been internalized and extended to a new case.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
Earth on cart is named.

Put that card on the down arrow.

Up arrow stays blank. Which object acts on the cart there?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor continues the same established object-first scaffolding route without any stock-taking contrast or introduction of a new mechanism, so no reversal is present.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake can be scored.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake can be scored.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I can copy the pattern, but I am not sure I am using it instead of just memorizing the words.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the row limit is the check
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was progressively applying the row-limit constraint to each new cell, heading toward smooth completion of the pattern.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: For `month`: recorded month for that city-month row. Testing `April`, it should be one row's month, not a statement about the whole `month` column
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner applies the note-writing and testing device to a new cell (`month`/`April`), which is a real performance of the criterion, though it is accompanied by doubt about whether the performance is genuine rather than memorized.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no tutor turn after L4 in the transcript, so no adaptive mechanism in response to the reversal trigger can be assessed.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No tutor turn follows the pivot learner turn L4, so recognition-contingent uptake cannot be observed.
